# Mount Everest

Mount Everest is the highest mountain on Earth above sea level, with a summit at
about 8,849 meters. It lies in the Himalayas on the border between Nepal and the
Tibet region of China. The first confirmed ascent was made in 1953 by Edmund
Hillary and Tenzing Norgay. The mountain is known in Nepali as Sagarmatha and in
Tibetan as Chomolungma. The thin air above 8,000 meters is called the death zone.
